Title: Synthetic healthy bounded baseline
Purpose: Verify installation, bounded current-state output, measured resource limits, and cleanup without asserting host-dependent counter values.

Arrange.Action: Install the framework and create a bounded synthetic database in the run-owned SQL Server container.
Arrange.CompletionSignal: The framework and synthetic database are online inside the registered container.

Act.Action: Execute a deterministic low-volume synthetic transaction.
Act.CompletionSignal: The transaction commits and the expected synthetic row count is present.

Observe.Scope: Only the registered synthetic database with sampling and deep options disabled.

AlternativeEvidence: AVAILABLE_LIMITED is accepted only when the returned module and warning contracts remain structurally valid.
ExpectedFindings.AssertionBoundary: Exact waits, durations, plans, CPU percentages, memory use, storage use, and row ordering are not asserted.

AlternativeEvidence: The Hyper-V Linux lane remains NOT_EXECUTED until an isolated compatible guest is available.

Cleanup.Action: Remove only the exact registered container and network after verifying their run labels, then remove the marker-protected run data directory.
Cleanup.CompletionSignal: No Docker object carrying the run identifier and no run-owned data directory remains.

KnownLimitations: The baseline proves a bounded SQL Server 2025 container path and does not represent production sizing.
External native-Linux and Hyper-V-Linux runtime evidence is tracked separately from repository validation.
